purpose: Top-level Git Manager view container — assembles RepoTabs across the top, a resizable left sidebar (ChangesPanel + CommitPanel), a draggable divider, the right content area with three tabs (Changes/DiffViewer, History/HistoryPanel, Branches/BranchPanel), plus a bottom RemotePanel and floating QuickActions bar. Manages the repo-discovery lifecycle: when activeRepo changes it (1) checks api.gitIsRepo to decide between GitWelcome (no repos), InitFlow (folder is not a git repo), or the full layout; (2) parallel-fetches initial data via Promise.all of gitStatus, gitBranches, gitCurrentBranch, gitLog, gitRemotes; (3) starts a 3-second gitStatus polling interval to keep the working-tree view live; (4) auto-fetches a diff for any pre-selected file (via GIT_NAVIGATE_TO_FILE flow from elsewhere in the app), tracking the last-fetched file in autoFetchedFileRef so manual selections in ChangesPanel are not double-fetched. Also implements the resizable sidebar drag handler (220–500 px range).
gotchas: isGitRepo has THREE states: null=unknown (still checking), true=valid repo, false=not a repo — render branches must handle all three (loading state for null, InitFlow for false, full UI for true).

The 3-second status polling interval continues for the lifetime of the active repo — switching tabs in the app doesn't unmount this view in some setups, so the polling persists. Always cleanup on activeRepo/isGitRepo changes via the useEffect cleanup.
autoFetchedFileRef.current prevents double-fetching a pre-selected file but is keyed by filename — selecting the same file twice in a row from elsewhere will only fetch once until the ref is reset.

The Ctrl/Cmd+Enter global shortcut focuses `.git-manager-commit-input` via document.querySelector — relies on a single instance of CommitPanel being in the DOM. Multiple instances will all receive the focus call.
Drag-resize attaches mousemove/mouseup listeners to document, NOT the divider — required for drag-outside-bounds behavior. Cleanup removes them and the body class .git-manager-dragging.

When activeRepo transitions from a valid repo back to null (close-tab), the gitStatus polling cleanup still fires but the dispatched data cleanup (clearing gitStatus etc.) is up to the GIT_CLOSE_REPO reducer — verify reducer handles this.
patterns: Three-mode render gate: `!hasRepos` → GitWelcome, `!isGitRepo` → InitFlow, otherwise full layout. Each mode reuses the RepoTabs header.
Parallel data load via Promise.all — five endpoints fire concurrently; each is null-checked in case the API surface evolves.
Refresh callback (refreshGitData) is passed to CommitPanel onCommitComplete — used to re-fetch everything after a commit lands.
DEFAULT_SIDEBAR_WIDTH (300) / MIN (220) / MAX (500) — clamp logic uses Math.min(MAX, Math.max(MIN, ...)).
conventions: All git API calls use window.electronAPI.git* and check `result?.success` before reading `result.data`.
Inline SVG icons (GitSpinnerIcon, DiffIcon, HistoryIcon, BranchIcon, GitRepoIcon) live at the bottom of the file.

depends_on: electron/preload.js (gitIsRepo, gitStatus, gitBranches, gitCurrentBranch, gitLog, gitRemotes, gitDiffFile)
